hero.title: TOMOCLOUD
hero.leadJa: 新しい自分が視える世界へ

vision.heading: Our Vision
vision.subtitleJa: 視える化技術により全ての人が自分らしく生きることのできる社会を創造する
vision.subtitleEn: Enabling authentic lives through the power of visualization.
vision.p1: For many people, continuously understanding their health in daily life is not easy.
vision.p2: There have also been limited means to easily monitor the body and tissues.
vision.p4: We aim to solve these challenges so everyone can live as their authentic selves.

ogawa.title:
Project Assistant Professor, Takei Lab, Graduate School of Engineering, Chiba University
Also EIR at IMO, Chiba University
ogawa.subtitle: Ph.D. (Engineering), Graduate School of Science and Engineering, Chiba University
ogawa.bio: Studied at the University of Edinburgh as a representative of Tobitate! Study Abroad JAPAN. Driving social implementation at the intersection of medicine and engineering, developing EIT-based devices to address lymphedema after gynecologic cancer treatment. Selected for JST START (2022) with Prof. Takei and team.
achievements: 2019: Selected as a Japan representative, World Youth Forum (under UN auspices)
2021: METI Japan Healthcare Business Contest – Excellence Award
2022: NEDO TCP Grand Prize; JSPS DC1; Chiba Univ. President/Dean/Chair Awards
2023: Early Ph.D. completion (5 papers); Best Presentation Award (Lymphedema Society); Research Studio; JSPS PD
2025: AMED Bridge (Tsukuba hub) S0; GTIE GAP Fund (Entry Course); Beyond Japan Zero to X

takei.positionNote: Professor, Graduate School of Engineering (Frontier Medical Engineering Center), Chiba University
takei.title: Professor, Graduate School of Engineering (Frontier Medical Engineering Center), Chiba University
takei.subtitle: Ph.D. in Engineering, Waseda University
takei.bio: Formerly at Nihon University (Assistant–Professor). Led research on electrical measurement and mechanics of multiphase flow, and advanced EIT fundamentals and applications (industrial/biomedical). At Chiba Univ., expanding to plant and biomedical measurement, visualizing thrombi in blood flow (KAKENHI A). Also selected for NEDO advanced IoT project (EIT & AI for lymphedema).
achievements: PI of JST A-STEP and multiple KAKENHI (A/B/C) grants
NEDO Advanced IoT Project (EIT×AI for lymphedema)
Invited talks at Lymphedema Society / JCS meetings
Multiple awards incl. NEDO special jury prize; NEDO TCP finalist

message.heading: Message
message.title: “Visualize the New Self”
message.p1: Even while working in an engineering lab, I spent my student years on hospital wards, listening directly to patients and clinicians. One patient said, “What frightens me most is that I can’t visualize what’s happening inside my body.” That stayed with me—and made me determined to build technology that eases that invisible anxiety. The more I listened, the more I felt these were not someone else’s problems, but challenges I should help solve.
message.p2: I also learned a simple, profound truth: patients are people leading full lives. Each person is at once a patient, a family member, a friend, a professional—many versions of “self” that existed before illness and will continue after. I want to support all of those selves. That’s why we shape technology not as a mere tool, but as a companion woven into everyday life.
message.p3: What we’ve established is the power of visualization through Electrical Impedance Tomography (EIT). Human tissues have distinct electrical properties, and those properties shift with fluid composition. EIT makes it possible to visualize the invisible—to render these properties clearly and non-invasively, with practical precision.
message.p4: With this, we can replace some costly, burdensome tools and enable at-home monitoring that used to be impossible to visualize—opening new possibilities for early detection and ongoing monitoring of conditions like lymphedema and other chronic diseases.
message.p5: This is not just visualization—it is a compass to see, understand, and take the next step. We aim to support that moment across healthcare, nursing care, and daily life, and to implement this Japan-born model globally.
